structure.affirmative: Subject + am/is/are + V-ing — She is working. / They are leaving.
structure.negative: Subject + am/is/are + not + V-ing — She isn't working. / They aren't leaving.
structure.question: Am/Is/Are + Subject + V-ing? — Is she working? / Are they leaving?

faq.q: What are 'stative verbs' and why can't they use -ing?
faq.a: Stative verbs describe states, not actions: know, love, want, believe, own. We say 'I know' not 'I am knowing'. Some verbs (think, have) can be stative or dynamic depending on meaning.

faq.q: What's the difference between 'She works' and 'She is working'?
faq.a: 'She works' = her general job/habit. 'She is working' = she is busy right now (at this exact moment or this period).

faq.q: Can Present Continuous describe the future?
faq.a: Yes, for personal arrangements already planned: 'I'm meeting Tom tonight'. This is different from 'will' (spontaneous) or 'going to' (intended plan).
